name: Integrated Circuit (IC)
tagline: Multiple transistors and components fabricated on a single semiconductor die
problem:
As circuits grow, wiring discrete components becomes a reliability and scale problem: solder joints fail, parasitics dominate, and assembly time explodes.

Integrated circuits solve this by fabricating many devices together on one substrate, shrinking size, improving reliability, and enabling complex systems.

The remaining problem for Integrated Circuit (IC) is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats integrated circuit (ic) as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
ICs use photolithography and thin-film processes to pattern transistors, interconnects, and passivation on silicon. Design and manufacturing are tightly coupled: yield and defect control matter as much as schematic logic.

For The Codex, ICs represent a major threshold: the required cleanliness, materials purity, and equipment are far beyond primitive industry, so early rebuild paths rely on importing ICs and building robust systems around them.

In collapse recovery, integrated circuit (ic) must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Photolithography
principles.explanation: Pattern transfer at small scales enables dense circuits.

principles.name: Planar processing
principles.explanation: Oxides and passivation stabilize surfaces and junctions.

principles.name: Yield and defects
principles.explanation: Complexity increases defect sensitivity; process control dominates economics.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Integrated Circuit (IC) is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

components.name: Input control point
components.function: Checks materials, tools, information, and environmental conditions before work starts
components.position: At receiving, storage, or setup area
components.madeFrom: Labels, inspection notes, gauges, samples, and quarantine bins
components.criticalNote: Unknown inputs should be isolated until checked.

rawMaterials.name: Silicon and dopants
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Import packaged ICs early; local IC fab requires cleanrooms, ultrapure chemicals, and precision tooling.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Ultrapure processing, lithography, and controlled diffusion/implant.

rawMaterials.name: Ultrapure chemicals
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Closed-loop chemical management is mandatory off-world; waste handling is safety-critical.

rawMaterials.name: Reference records and operating notes
rawMaterials.purpose: Preserves the local method, limits, measurements, and revisions for Integrated Circuit (IC)
earthLocations: Libraries, workshops, clinics, schools, field notebooks, maintenance logs, and printed manuals
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Keep redundant digital and printed copies with checksums, revision history, and offline access inside the habitat.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Label version, author, date, assumptions, measurement units, and known failure cases.

buildSteps.title: Design for manufacturability
buildSteps.description: Choose process node constraints; design test structures and margins.
buildSteps.warningNote: A design that cannot be tested is not a product.

buildSteps.title: Fabricate wafers
buildSteps.description: Run lithography, doping, deposition, and etch steps with strict process logs.
buildSteps.warningNote: Toxic and corrosive chemicals require professional safety systems.

buildSteps.title: Test and package
buildSteps.description: Probe wafers, dice, package, and perform burn-in screening.
buildSteps.warningNote: ESD and thermal overstress can destroy devices.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Integrated Circuit (IC) is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

buildSteps.title: Inspect inputs before use
buildSteps.description: Check materials, tools, water, power, workspace, records, and operator readiness. Mark substitutions and unknowns before work begins.
buildSteps.warningNote: Substitutions can introduce contamination, fire, structural, electrical, medical, or chemical hazards.

history.event: MOS and scaling drive complexity growth.
Multi-layer interconnect and advanced nodes dominate.

impact:
ICs enable computers, communications, and control systems at scales impossible with discrete components. They also create global supply-chain dependencies on specialized manufacturing.

In The Codex, ICs are both a pinnacle manufacturing node and a dependency anchor for advanced computing and AI.

sources.title: Sze & Ng — Physics of Semiconductor Devices
